automotive-ev-battery.title: China achieves record NEV share – exports drive growth
automotive-ev-battery.summary: China recorded nearly 1.5 million sales of battery-electric vehicles and plug-in hybrids in May, with NEVs accounting for a record 56.9% of total vehicle wholesale sales. The figures indicate that exports, rather than domestic demand, are driving growth in the world's largest EV market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD: World's Largest Automaker In 5 Years
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD Chairman Wang Chuanfu indicated that the company will become the world's largest automaker by volume within five years and exceed 10 million units by the end of the decade. The announcement underscores BYD's aggressive expansion strategy in the global EV market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Green light for the EU's 'Battery Booster'
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The European Commission has launched its Battery Booster Facility to support battery cell production in Europe, providing €1.5 billion in interest-free loans. The initiative aims to strengthen Europe's battery manufacturing capacity and reduce dependence on non-EU suppliers.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mercedes-Benz begins axial flux motor production in Berlin
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes-Benz has commenced series production of its axial-flux electric motor at its Berlin-Marienfelde plant following its 2021 acquisition of British specialist Yasa. The move represents a significant step in bringing advanced motor technology into mainstream EV production.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD's Flash Charging expected to cost Under 60 cent/kWh
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD is preparing to enter the European fast-charging market with its Flash charging stations, targeting pricing below 50 pence per kilowatt-hour (approximately 58 cents). The company's expansion into European charging infrastructure could intensify competition in the EV charging sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: The Rivian R2 Is Here!
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Rivian has begun public customer deliveries of its R2 midsize SUV, marking the company's entry into the mass-market EV segment after years of development. The launch of the more affordable R2 represents a crucial expansion of Rivian's addressable market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Stellantis tests autonomous ride-hailing service in Luxembourg
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Stellantis, Bolt, and Pony.ai have launched an autonomous mobility pilot programme in Luxembourg focusing on autonomous vehicles and their integration into ride-hailing platforms. The 'Living Lab' initiative aims to align regulatory requirements with autonomous EV deployment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Milence opens first HGV charging hub in Denmark
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Milence has opened its first heavy-goods vehicle charging hub in Padborg on the Danish-German border, featuring four CCS charging points with up to 400 kW capacity. The facility addresses critical charging infrastructure needs for electric freight transport between Scandinavia and Central Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Solaris delivers 40 electric buses to MZK Toruń
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Polish public transport operator MZK Toruń has added 40 Solaris Urbino 12 electric buses to its fleet along with associated charging infrastructure. The contract reflects continued European momentum in transitioning public transport to zero-emission vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electric Airplane Powered By Solid-State Batteries Completes Test Flight
automotive-ev-battery.summary: An electric airplane powered by solid-state batteries has successfully completed a test flight, demonstrating the practical viability of next-generation battery technology. The milestone suggests solid-state batteries may soon become available for commercial EV applications.

world-news.title: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait and ships in Hormuz after fresh US strikes
world-news.summary: Iran has launched drone strikes against military bases in Bahrain and Kuwait and targeted vessels in the Strait of Hormuz following new US military strikes on Iranian targets including Qeshm Island and coastal ports. The escalation marks a significant intensification of US-Iran hostilities with direct implications for global energy security and regional stability.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Tehran targets Bahrain, Kuwaiti bases after new US strikes
world-news.summary: Iran has announced a complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all vessels in response to continued US military operations, while claiming strikes on military installations in the Gulf region. The blockade of a critical global shipping chokepoint poses major risks to international energy markets and maritime trade.

world-news.title: Iran announces closure of Strait of Hormuz following US strikes
world-news.summary: Iranian officials announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most critical energy chokepoints through which approximately one-third of global seaborne oil passes, in response to US military strikes. This blockade could have severe consequences for international energy prices and global trade.

world-news.title: Tehran targets US Gulf bases after American forces launch new strikes on Iran
world-news.summary: US Central Command conducted military strikes against Iranian targets in response to what it characterized as "unwarranted and continued aggression" from Iran. The escalating exchange of strikes between the two nations represents a critical moment in US-Iran tensions with potential regional ramifications.

world-news.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
world-news.summary: Israeli military strikes in southern Lebanon, including a series of attacks on the town of Tayr Debba, resulted in at least 17 deaths according to Lebanese state news sources. The incidents underscore ongoing tensions between Israel and Hezbollah along the Lebanon-Israel border.

world-news.title: Three Indian sailors missing after US says it hit tanker in Gulf of Oman
world-news.summary: Three Indian sailors are missing and 21 crew members were rescued after the Settebello tanker was struck in the Gulf of Oman, which the US confirmed it had targeted. The incident raises questions about civilian maritime safety amid escalating military operations in strategic shipping lanes.

world-news.title: Canada introduces bill to ban social media for children under 16
world-news.summary: Canada has introduced legislation to restrict social media access for minors under 16 and establish a digital regulator to enforce safety standards for AI chatbots. The proposed bill represents a significant policy approach to digital regulation that could influence international technology governance.

world-news.title: Hegseth: Guantanamo Bay prisoners 'should have been executed'
world-news.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated that prisoners held at Guantanamo Bay should have been executed, comments that reflect the Trump administration's position on detention and interrogation policy. The statement signals a potential shift in US policy regarding detainee treatment and international humanitarian law considerations.

world-news.title: Trump's claim of escorting oil through Hormuz won't ease crisis
world-news.summary: Analysis suggests that President Trump's proposal to escort oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz is insufficient to resolve the current geopolitical crisis stemming from US-Iran tensions. The assessment highlights the complexity of maintaining global energy supply chains amid escalating Middle Eastern instability.

world-news.title: Trump says he 'loves the inflation' as US prices rise at fastest rate in three years
world-news.summary: US inflation has accelerated to its fastest rate in three years, with President Trump expressing unusual support for rising prices during a period of economic strain. The statement reflects divergent views on macroeconomic policy and has implications for US consumer purchasing power and global economic stability.

tech-ai.title: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims
tech-ai.summary: A former xAI engineer has filed a lawsuit against the company and SpaceX, alleging he was terminated for raising safety concerns about the Grok AI system days before SpaceX's anticipated IPO. The case highlights tensions between AI safety advocacy and corporate interests during a critical business milestone.

tech-ai.title: North Koreans behind nearly half of US tech industry hacks, says CrowdStrike
tech-ai.summary: North Korean-backed hackers impersonating remote IT workers and recruiters have been responsible for approximately half of all cyberattacks targeting U.S., European, and Asian technology companies over the past 12 months. The threat actors continue to pose a significant and persistent risk to corporate infrastructure globally.

tech-ai.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
tech-ai.summary: Amazon has secured a $17.5 billion loan from banks following a bond offering, reflecting the company's continued aggressive investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure. The borrowing underscores the substantial financial commitments technology companies are making to maintain competitive positions in the AI arms race.

tech-ai.title: Cybercriminals claim breach of Oracle PeopleSoft servers at 100-plus organizations
tech-ai.summary: The ShinyHunters hacking group claims to have compromised Oracle PeopleSoft servers across more than 100 organizations, including numerous universities, potentially exposing sensitive corporate and educational data. The alleged breach represents a significant security incident affecting critical enterprise resource planning systems.

tech-ai.title: 'AI-pilled' firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
tech-ai.summary: According to Ramp's AI Index, companies most aggressively pursuing artificial intelligence investments are spending approximately $7,500 monthly per employee on AI-related expenses. The substantial spending reflects the competitive intensity of the AI sector, though costs currently remain below typical engineer salaries.

tech-ai.title: Everyone wants a piece of Tesla's battery business
tech-ai.summary: Electricity demand from AI data centers is driving major automakers including General Motors and Ford to enter the energy storage business, intensifying competition in battery technology and infrastructure. The trend reflects how AI's power requirements are reshaping the broader technology and automotive industries.

tech-ai.title: Nearly a million passports and photo IDs were left unprotected on the public internet
tech-ai.summary: Approximately one million passport and identity document images were discovered exposed on publicly accessible internet URLs without password protection or access controls, creating a significant data security and privacy crisis. The incident underscores vulnerabilities in data storage infrastructure and the ease with which sensitive personal information can be inadvertently exposed.

tech-ai.title: The Indian government got cold feet on Starlink just before SpaceX's IPO
tech-ai.summary: Regulatory complications surrounding SpaceX's Starlink expansion in India have emerged at a critical moment ahead of the company's anticipated initial public offering. The delays in India's market could undermine growth projections that were central to SpaceX's IPO narrative.

tech-ai.title: Apple's new Siri AI knows when to shut up
tech-ai.summary: Apple's new Siri AI assistant is designed with a deliberately concise personality that avoids excessive verbosity, distinguishing it from other AI chatbots that adopt cheerier and more conversational tones. The restrained approach reflects Apple's philosophy of providing functional responses without fostering potentially unhealthy user attachment.

tech-ai.title: Xbox warns of a 'reset' as it prepares for layoffs
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft's Xbox division faces significant layoffs next month, with reports suggesting the cuts could involve studio closures or restructuring of the gaming division's portfolio. The restructuring, hinted at by Xbox CEO Asha Sharma, represents a strategic recalibration for the company's gaming business.

science-space.title: NASA Webb Finds Strongest Evidence Yet for 'Black Hole Stars'
science-space.summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has identified compelling evidence supporting the existence of "black hole stars" by analyzing the spectrum of a mysterious cosmic object known as a "little red dot." The discovery, led by astronomers at the University of Texas at Austin, helps resolve a long-standing puzzle about early universe objects detected since 2022.

science-space.title: NASA's Ambitious Artemis 3 Mission Includes 3 Giant Rocket Launches, 2 Private Moon Landers and 1 Big Question: Can It All Work Together?
science-space.summary: NASA's Artemis 3 mission planned for 2027 represents one of the agency's most complex undertakings, requiring three separate giant rocket launches and relying on two private lunar lander prototypes that remain under development. The mission's success depends on unprecedented coordination among multiple systems and partners, raising significant technical and logistical questions.

science-space.title: NASA is Building a New Space Telescope to Search for Life on Nearby Planets. What Would It See on Ancient Earth?
science-space.summary: NASA's forthcoming Habitable Worlds Observatory will be equipped to detect biosignatures in the atmospheres of distant exoplanets, with new research analyzing how effectively it could identify signs of life on an ancient Earth. The study provides crucial benchmarks for the telescope's capability to revolutionize the search for extraterrestrial life.

science-space.title: Train Ride to NASA Kennedy for Artemis III Booster Segments
science-space.summary: The final eight booster motor segments for NASA's Space Launch System rocket have shipped from Northrop Grumman's facility in Utah to Kennedy Space Center in Florida as part of preparations for the Artemis III mission. The delivery marks a significant milestone in assembling the hardware needed for NASA's crewed lunar mission.

science-space.title: NASA's CloudCube Pioneers Miniaturized Radar to Study Clouds, Precipitation
science-space.summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory has developed CloudCube, a compact multifrequency radar instrument that simultaneously transmits three radar signals to provide enhanced sensitivity to various water droplets and ice particles in cloud systems. The innovation promises to improve data collection on dynamic atmospheric phenomena across a broader range of scales.

science-space.title: NASA Equips Astronauts, Industry with Robotic Intelligence
science-space.summary: NASA is advancing robotic technology to support long-duration lunar missions by enabling robots to perform routine tasks and free astronauts for scientific work. A Boulder-based robotics company is addressing key challenges in robotic decision-making and object recognition to support NASA's ambitious exploration objectives.

science-space.title: The Asteroid That Wiped Out the Dinosaurs May Have Created a Vast Underground Habitat for Life That Lasted 8 Million Years
science-space.summary: New research suggests that the Chicxulub asteroid impact that caused dinosaur extinction paradoxically created favorable conditions for subsurface microbial life, potentially sustaining a vast underground biosphere for approximately 8 million years. The discovery highlights how catastrophic events can inadvertently produce unexpected habitats for organisms.

science-space.title: Curiosity Blog: Sols 4913-4919: Planetary Explorers, Freewheeling to the Yardang Unit
science-space.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover continues its ongoing mission on Mars, transitioning between defined science campaigns and opportunistic exploration as it moves toward a new geological unit called the Yardang feature. The rover's flexible operational approach allows scientists to maximize discoveries during long-duration planetary surface missions.

science-space.title: Tyndall's Trail of Bergs
science-space.summary: Satellite imagery documents the ongoing calving of ice from southern Patagonia's glaciers into an expanding glacial lake, providing visual evidence of glacial dynamics and climate-driven changes in the region. The phenomenon demonstrates the active nature of polar ice systems and their response to environmental conditions.

science-space.title: Swirling Spiral Galaxy Will Hypnotize You | Space Photo of the Day for June 10, 2026
science-space.summary: The Hubble Space Telescope has captured a detailed image of Messier 88 (NGC 4501), a spiral galaxy showcasing the intricate structure of galactic arms and stellar populations. The photograph highlights the continued capabilities of Hubble in revealing the fine details of distant galaxies.

finance-markets.title: US inflation jumped to 4.2% in May amid Middle East energy shock
finance-markets.summary: US inflation rose to 4.2% in May, marking a new three-year high as Middle East tensions disrupted energy supplies and pressured prices upward. The sharp increase signals persistent inflationary pressures despite Federal Reserve efforts to control price growth, with geopolitical risks now compounding monetary policy challenges.

finance-markets.title: Oil Surges as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Threaten Fragile Truce
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices surged following renewed US military strikes on Iran, with the Islamic Republic announcing a halt to all vessel traffic through the strategically critical Strait of Hormuz in retaliation. The escalation threatens a fragile ceasefire and poses substantial risks to global energy supplies and commodity markets.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX IPO Is Said to Be Over Four Times Oversubscribed
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX's initial public offering has attracted investor demand exceeding four times the available shares, signalling exceptional market appetite for the aerospace company's stock. The strong oversubscription reflects robust investor confidence in the commercial space sector and high-growth technology ventures.

finance-markets.title: Trump suggests he may not renew trade deal with Mexico and Canada
finance-markets.summary: President Trump indicated possible non-renewal of the 2020 trilateral trade agreement with Mexico and Canada, stating the US has no need for goods from its neighbours. The remark signals potential trade policy uncertainty and could trigger significant market volatility across North American supply chains and financial markets.

finance-markets.title: Oracle to spend $70bn on data centre build-out in coming year
finance-markets.summary: Oracle announced plans to invest $70 billion in data centre expansion over the coming year, reflecting the tech giant's strategic pivot toward artificial intelligence and cloud infrastructure. However, shares fell as much as 8% in after-hours trading following guidance for flat revenue, signalling investor concerns about near-term profitability.

finance-markets.title: US tech stocks sink as volatility flares up on Wall Street
finance-markets.summary: US technology stocks declined sharply as market volatility intensified ahead of SpaceX's historic IPO, with profit-taking and valuation concerns weighing on previously high-flying names. The selloff reflects broader investor caution amid macroeconomic uncertainties and mixed earnings outlooks in the sector.

finance-markets.title: Trump's Iran war has propelled China's cleantech industry
finance-markets.summary: Middle East tensions and potential disruptions to global energy supplies have accelerated demand for renewable energy technologies, benefiting China's cleantech sector significantly. The geopolitical crisis is inadvertently catalysing the global energy transition and reshaping competitive dynamics in alternative energy markets.

finance-markets.title: Danantara Starts Bond Sale in Test After Indonesian Selloff
finance-markets.summary: Indonesia's sovereign wealth fund Danantara launched its debut global dollar bond sale, testing market confidence in Southeast Asia's largest economy at a time when investor sentiment has deteriorated. The offering's reception will signal the depth of recovery in regional credit markets and broader emerging-market appetite.

finance-markets.title: Investor Tied to Troubled Bank Loans Charged With Fraud by US
finance-markets.summary: A California real estate investor has been criminally charged by US authorities in connection with an alleged $100 million fraud scheme involving Western Alliance Bancorp. The charges represent continued fallout from a troubled commercial property portfolio that sparked systemic concerns at multiple financial institutions.

finance-markets.title: Ashley's Frasers Seeks to Buy Rest of Boss for $2.3 Billion
finance-markets.summary: Frasers Group has made an offer to acquire the remaining stake in Hugo Boss AG for approximately €2 billion ($2.3 billion), as billionaire Mike Ashley expands his fashion retail empire. The proposed acquisition reflects consolidation trends in the apparel sector and Ashley's aggressive portfolio diversification strategy.

crypto-web3.title: Prediction markets get first U.S. rule proposal as CFTC pursues contract reviews
crypto-web3.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has opened a proposed rule for public comment that establishes a framework for determining which prediction market contracts serve the public interest. This represents the first regulatory guidance from U.S. authorities specifically addressing the prediction markets sector.

crypto-web3.title: BlackRock and Fidelity are quietly turning bitcoin ETFs into a two-firm market
crypto-web3.summary: BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC are capturing the overwhelming majority of inflows into bitcoin ETFs, consolidating institutional demand among the two largest asset managers. Smaller competitors are increasingly marginalized as institutional investors gravitate toward the largest players in the market.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase-backed Stand With Crypto calls on members to campaign against banks blocking digital asset transactions
crypto-web3.summary: Stand With Crypto UK is mobilizing its members to file formal complaints with high-street banks over widespread transfer bans affecting the crypto sector. The campaign reflects growing frustration among industry advocates over banking sector restrictions limiting access to digital asset services.

crypto-web3.title: Mastercard unveils Agent Pay for Machines to support autonomous AI transactions, including stablecoins
crypto-web3.summary: Mastercard has introduced Agent Pay for Machines infrastructure designed to facilitate high-volume, low-value payments by autonomous AI agents using stablecoins and other digital assets. The initiative positions the payments giant to capture transaction volume from AI-driven commerce ecosystems.

crypto-web3.title: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
crypto-web3.summary: A recent Citi research note concludes that quantum computing poses a significant long-term threat specifically to Bitcoin security that should concern institutional holders. The analysis contrasts Bitcoin's vulnerability with Ethereum's relative resilience to quantum risks.

crypto-web3.title: Solana infrastructure firm Helius acquires Light Protocol to expand onchain privacy
crypto-web3.summary: Helius has acquired Light Protocol to enhance privacy capabilities within the Solana ecosystem, reflecting broader industry consolidation and renewed focus on onchain privacy solutions. The acquisition positions Helius to offer more robust privacy-preserving infrastructure to Solana developers and users.

crypto-web3.title: Kalshi now requires users to reveal employers as it fights insider trading and market manipulation
crypto-web3.summary: The prediction markets platform Kalshi has implemented new disclosure requirements mandating users reveal their employers for markets deemed to carry elevated insider trading and manipulation risks. The measure represents an industry effort to strengthen compliance and prevent conflicts of interest in prediction markets.

crypto-web3.title: Netomi CEO says $5 trillion AI customer experience market could boost stablecoin demand
crypto-web3.summary: Netomi's CEO, a former Wall Street engineer, argues that the expanding AI enterprise software market will substantially increase stablecoin and blockchain adoption for commerce transactions. The projection highlights potential demand catalysts for digital assets as AI agents require settlement infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Privacy returns to focus as Ethereum developers explore new token standards
crypto-web3.summary: Ethereum developers are actively exploring new token standards with enhanced privacy features as the ecosystem prioritizes privacy protections. The initiative reflects growing demand for privacy-preserving solutions among Ethereum stakeholders.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum could become a fully zero-knowledge proof-based protocol in 3 to 5 years, Joe Lubin says
crypto-web3.summary: Ethereum co-founder Joe Lubin stated that the network could transition to a fully zero-knowledge proof-based architecture within three to five years to achieve the infinite scalability required for a global computing platform. The shift would rely on Layer 2 solutions and advanced cryptographic techniques to handle unlimited transaction capacity.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We're reading about U.S. pressure on European drug prices, longer shortages, and more
health-biotech.summary: A new analysis reveals that the average drug shortage in 2025 lasted 5.3 years, a significant increase from 4.3 years in 2024, indicating a worsening crisis in pharmaceutical supply chains. The report also examines U.S. pressure on European drug pricing and its implications for global pharmaceutical markets.

health-biotech.title: Are Republicans turning against medications for treating opioid addiction?
health-biotech.summary: Federal actions and policy proposals have reignited debate over methadone and buprenorphine treatments for opioid use disorder, raising concerns among addiction experts and patient advocacy groups. The political shift threatens access to evidence-based medications that are critical to combating the opioid epidemic.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: As the U.S. looks on, European countries feel growing pressure on drug prices
health-biotech.summary: European nations are adopting divergent approaches to address pharmaceutical pricing as U.S. pressure intensifies, creating competing paths that will significantly impact both drug manufacturers and patient access. The divergence raises questions about the influence of U.S. policies on global drug pricing strategies.

health-biotech.title: Exclusive: Genentech closes infectious disease unit in wave of research layoffs
health-biotech.summary: Roche's Genentech has undertaken significant layoffs in its early-stage research and development division, including the closure of two research units and departure of senior researchers. The move reflects ongoing consolidation in the biotech industry as companies reassess their research priorities.

health-biotech.title: Sanofi to mull riliprubart's future after stopping late-stage nerve disease study
health-biotech.summary: Sanofi has halted a Phase 3 clinical trial of its neurological disorder antibody drug after an independent review committee determined the study was unlikely to demonstrate sufficient efficacy. The company is now evaluating the future of the riliprubart program following the setback.

health-biotech.title: OB-GYN association, deviating from CDC guidance, issues its own vaccine recommendations
health-biotech.summary: The American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists has released its own recommended vaccine schedule for pregnant patients that diverges from CDC guidance, raising questions about clinical consistency and public health messaging. The separate recommendations could impact vaccination rates and patient decision-making during pregnancy.

health-biotech.title: Ethyreal lands $101M to treat both Graves' and thyroid eye disease
health-biotech.summary: A newly launched biotech backed by Atlas Venture and Medicxi has secured $101 million in funding to develop a novel treatment for Graves' disease and thyroid eye disease through a differentiated mechanism of action. The company plans to enter clinical trials by year's end.

health-biotech.title: SonoThera raises $125M for non-viral gene therapy approach
health-biotech.summary: SonoThera has raised $125 million to advance its ultrasound-based gene therapy platform into clinical development, with lead programs targeting Duchenne muscular dystrophy and autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease. The non-viral approach represents a novel delivery mechanism for gene therapy applications.

health-biotech.title: Parabilis CEO Mathai Mammen on biotech's record IPO
health-biotech.summary: Parabilis Medicines completed the largest biotech initial public offering in industry history with a $670 million Nasdaq debut, marking a significant milestone for the Cambridge-based drug developer. The substantial capital raise signals investor confidence in emerging biotech companies.

health-biotech.title: Novartis and Orionis strike second molecular glue deal for $40M upfront
health-biotech.summary: Novartis has expanded its partnership with Orionis Biosciences with a second molecular glue deal valued at $40 million upfront and up to $1.4 billion in milestone payments. The expanded collaboration demonstrates continued confidence in the therapeutic potential of molecular glue degrader technology.

startups-vc.title: Jedify raises $24M to help companies arm AI agents with context on their business
startups-vc.summary: Jedify has secured $24 million in Series funding led by Norwest, with participation from S Capital VC, Cerca Partners, Oceans Ventures, and Snowflake Ventures as a strategic investor. The platform helps enterprises equip AI agents with contextual business information, addressing a critical need in the rapidly expanding enterprise AI market.

startups-vc.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
startups-vc.summary: Niteshift, a new AI coding agent startup founded by former Datadog veterans, has raised $7 million in seed funding from a prominent group of angel investors. The company is positioning itself against vendor lock-in by major AI model providers, offering enterprises greater control and flexibility over their AI-powered development tools.

startups-vc.title: Sector Snapshot: Semiconductor Startup Funding Still Running Hot
startups-vc.summary: The semiconductor startup sector continues to attract substantial investor capital, with approximately $10 billion deployed into seed through pre-IPO rounds in 2026 to date. This sustained funding momentum reflects continued investor confidence in the sector despite broader market volatility and demonstrates semiconductor's strategic importance to the technology ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: Decart's new world model can simulate hours of photorealistic driving — with some caveats
startups-vc.summary: Decart has launched Oasis 3, a real-time world model capable of generating photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing, now available via API for developers. The technology represents a significant advancement in AV simulation infrastructure, though the company notes certain limitations in the current implementation.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: Scotch Raises $20M Series A To Disrupt Legacy Liquor Retail Tech With AI
startups-vc.summary: Scotch, a Denver-based AI-native operating system for liquor retailers, has raised $20 million in Series A funding to modernize point-of-sale and management systems for independent and chain liquor stores. The startup targets a fragmented market characterized by legacy technology and operational inefficiency.

startups-vc.title: Why enterprise AI will be a major focus at VivaTech 2026
startups-vc.summary: While Silicon Valley continues emphasizing consumer-facing AI and large language models, European companies are prioritizing the application of AI to complex legacy systems embedded in everyday business operations. This geographic divergence in AI strategy reflects different market maturity levels and enterprise software adoption patterns.

startups-vc.title: Zest launches a restaurant discovery app powered by where people actually eat
startups-vc.summary: Zest, backed by Alexis Ohanian's 776 and Kindred Ventures, has launched a restaurant discovery application that leverages transaction data and AI to generate personalized dining recommendations based on users' actual eating patterns and frequented establishments. The approach addresses limitations in traditional review-based recommendation systems.

startups-vc.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
startups-vc.summary: Warner Music Group has acquired Sureel AI to strengthen its ability to track and monitor the use of artists' work in AI-generated content and model training datasets. The acquisition underscores major media companies' growing focus on protecting intellectual property rights in the era of generative AI.

startups-vc.title: Andrew Yang on Noble Mobile, UBI, and why he's done waiting for policy to catch up
startups-vc.summary: Former presidential candidate Andrew Yang discusses his shift from policy advocacy to entrepreneurship, launching ventures like Noble Mobile while ideas once considered fringe—such as Universal Basic Income—have gained mainstream traction from AI leaders and politicians. Yang's move reflects broader recognition that technological disruption may require business solutions alongside policy changes.

startups-vc.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
startups-vc.summary: Over 127,000 workers at U.S.-based tech companies experienced mass layoffs in 2025, with workforce reductions continuing into 2026, according to Crunchbase News data. The tracker provides ongoing visibility into structural employment changes within the technology sector amid evolving market conditions.

energy-climate.title: Guest post: How a record-high 'energy imbalance' is driving global warming
energy-climate.summary: Scientists report that the planet's energy imbalance has reached record levels, accelerating global warming beyond previous projections. The imbalance between incoming solar radiation and outgoing heat represents a critical measure of climate change acceleration.

energy-climate.title: AI boom means US is now 'investing more' in fossil-fuel power than China
energy-climate.summary: The rapid expansion of data centers to support artificial intelligence is driving unprecedented investment in natural gas infrastructure in the United States, surpassing Chinese fossil fuel investment. This trend raises concerns about emissions growth despite renewable energy expansion.

energy-climate.title: US government restricts trade with prominent Chinese storage companies
energy-climate.summary: The US Department of Defense has imposed trade restrictions on major Chinese battery and solar companies including CATL, BYD, and JA Solar, effective from 2027. The restrictions target critical supply chains in energy storage and renewable manufacturing.

energy-climate.title: Poland's new energy strategy envisages sharp increase in renewables
energy-climate.summary: Poland's updated National Energy and Climate Plan accelerates its energy transition through 2040, prioritizing renewable energy expansion and grid modernization. The strategy positions the country as a significant player in European decarbonization efforts.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 29 May 2026: Europe's 'mind-boggling' May | Indian heat deaths | Nigeria's solar mini-grids
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers extreme weather impacts across Europe and India alongside emerging renewable solutions in Nigeria. The report highlights the increasing urgency of climate adaptation and renewable deployment in vulnerable regions.

energy-climate.title: New York hits 5.6 GW hourly solar generation record
energy-climate.summary: New York's electric system achieved a record 5.6 GW hourly solar generation, reflecting rapid renewable capacity growth. However, grid operators warn that the system is operating with the narrowest reliability margins in recent years, raising concerns about grid stability.

energy-climate.title: Production not politics, capex not capacity – the new metrics defining U.S. solar manufacturing
energy-climate.summary: The U.S. solar manufacturing sector is transitioning from a niche focus to mainstream industry scrutiny, with emphasis shifting toward actual production output and capital expenditure rather than theoretical capacity. This evolution reflects the industry's maturation and growing importance to national energy security.

energy-climate.title: What new redispatching rules and negative prices mean for German renewable asset management
energy-climate.summary: Germany's renewable energy market is undergoing structural changes with increased grid volatility, negative pricing events, and new redispatching rules that complicate asset management. These shifts require operators to adopt more granular operational strategies beyond traditional monthly revenue reporting.

energy-climate.title: Foam-based floating PV system for cold climates
energy-climate.summary: Canadian researchers have demonstrated a foam-backed floating solar system that effectively operates through freezing winters while producing 2.7% more annual energy than conventional panels. The technology shows strong economic viability with approximately 4.2-year payback periods under favorable electricity pricing.

energy-climate.title: Pakistan targets solar sector with higher sales tax on modules, increased duties on batteries and inverters
energy-climate.summary: Pakistan is implementing tax increases on solar modules and batteries that threaten to dampen rapid growth in the solar sector despite strong expansion in recent years. Industry groups warn that the measures could impede battery energy storage system deployment critical for grid stability.

cybersecurity.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

cybersecurity.title: China-Linked JDY Botnet Expands to 1,500+ Devices for Cyber Reconnaissance
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "resurgence and expansion" of JDY, a covert network associated with China-nexus state-sponsored threat actors.

"The JDY botnet comprises over 1,500 SOHO [small office and home office] and IoT devices and operates as a centrally controlled, high-performance scanner used to discover, fingerprint, and continuously map exposed services at scale," Lumen's

cybersecurity.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
cybersecurity.summary:
Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure.

The security flaw patched by Fortinet relates to a command injection vulnerability in FortiSandbox, FortiSandbox Cloud, and FortiSandbox PaaS WEB UI. It's tracked as CVE-2026-25089 (CVSS score: 9.1).

cybersecurity.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
cybersecurity.summary:
A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to findings from VulnCheck.

The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-5027 (CVSS score: 8.8), a case of path traversal that could allow an attacker to write files to arbitrary locations.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Adds Cisco, Chrome, and Arista Flaws to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
cybersecurity.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation.

The list of vulnerabilities is as follows -


  CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8) - An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that could allow an

cybersecurity.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
cybersecurity.summary:
Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem.

Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report looks stable. Leadership reads "stable" as "secure." It usually isn't. The work slows down. The risk does not.

That gap is what a The Hacker News webinar with Picus Security sets out to close.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
cybersecurity.summary:
Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release.

Of the 206 flaws, 39 are rated Critical, and 167 are rated Important in severity. This includes 63 privilege escalation, 56 remote code execution, 30 information disclosure, 27 spoofing, 20 security

asia-pacific.title: As Japanese and Korean giants move upmarket, China’s MLCC firms see their chance
asia-pacific.summary:
As demand for AI servers soars, leading Japanese and South Korean multilayer ceramic capacitor (MLCC) makers are shifting further upmarket, opening the door for Chinese competitors to gain share in lower-end segments despite lagging their rivals in advanced technology.
MLCCs – often referred to as the “rice of the electronics industry” because of their ubiquity – are tiny electronic components that regulate and stabilise electrical currents in devices ranging from smartphones and electric...

asia-pacific.title: ESF Unveils Guaranteed Pathway, Completing Through-Train Model
asia-pacific.summary:
[The content of this article has been produced by our advertising partner.]
In Hong Kong, few conversations among parents are as charged as the one about school places. From the moment a child turns three, families begin plotting pathways in an already competitive system.
But what if that uncertainty could be removed at the very start?
The English Schools Foundation (ESF) announced this week that it will complete its “through-train” education model by formally linking its kindergartens with...

asia-pacific.title: Was Xi’s stance on China-North Korea military ties also a message for US, Russia?
asia-pacific.summary:
North Korea is gaining in strategic importance for China to counter the United States, but Pyongyang may refrain from intensifying military ties with Beijing, analysts say.
During his meeting with North Korean leader Kim Jong-un on Monday, Chinese President Xi Jinping said both sides should “enhance exchanges in diplomacy, law enforcement and military affairs”, according to state news agency Xinhua.
Despite pledges from both sides to strengthen strategic communications, denuclearisation of the..

asia-pacific.title: The Chinese AI police tech aimed at physical, psychological and emotional states
asia-pacific.summary:
Chinese AI-enabled equipment can help police assess the physical health, mental state, and even risk level of suspects, according to demonstrations at a law enforcement equipment exhibition in Beijing last week.
Chinese firms presenting their latest biometric devices at the international police and anti-terrorism technology expo said they could reduce manpower requirements for a police force and improve efficiency amid a shortage of frontline officers.
The three-day exhibit which ended on...

asia-pacific.title: Baby-sized Chinese man, 25, goes viral online after younger sister shares caregiving journey
asia-pacific.summary:
Wang Junming from central China’s Hunan province may be 25 years old, but he is still the size of a baby.
Standing at 66cm tall, Wang has the IQ of a child. He is spoiled by his family, including his 21-year-old sister, who all call him “baby”.
He need not worry about graduation and jobs as his peers do and he sleeps before 8pm every day.
The eye pouches and wrinkles on his face and the characters that pop out from his mouth make it is hard to believe that Wang is a baby.

He went viral after...

asia-pacific.title: Why Hong Kong should be very interested in the Pinglu Canal
asia-pacific.summary:
Our report that China’s landmark Pinglu Canal is set for trial operations in September was one of the most read the week it was published.
Interestingly, most of those who read the report were from the United States, followed by Malaysia, Singapore, Canada, Australia and Hong Kong. The report had three times as many Singaporean readers as local ones. I find this disparity concerning and not for reasons of editorial vanity.
While the Pinglu Canal is a global story – representing one of the...

asia-pacific.title: Kazakhstan bank turns to Hong Kong as gateway to offshore renminbi finance
asia-pacific.summary:
A Kazakhstan-based commercial bank backed by China Citic Bank is seeking to collaborate with Hong Kong financial institutions and investors to develop new financing channels for the Central Asian country through the city’s thriving offshore renminbi market.
Murat Baisynov, chairman of Altyn Bank, told the South China Morning Post that three clients of the Almaty-headquartered lender were currently exploring the issuance of dim sum bonds in Hong Kong, a trend he expected to continue.
“Hong Kong..

asia-pacific.title: BYD powers past Geely as oil shock charges up global EV demand
asia-pacific.summary:
The global energy crisis has helped propel BYD back into pole position in China’s automotive sector, allowing it to regain ground on Geely Auto as demand for battery-powered vehicles accelerates worldwide.
The Shenzhen-based electric vehicle (EV) maker, which lost its title as mainland China’s largest carmaker in the first quarter of 2026, rebounded strongly in the following two months as the US-Israel war with Iran drove up oil prices. Its overseas deliveries surged 76 per cent year on year to.

asia-pacific.title: Philippine military chief assures no political intervention amid Senate turmoil
asia-pacific.summary:
The Philippines’ armed forces chief has moved to quell fears of political intervention after a bitter Senate leadership crisis triggered tighter security, stalled military promotions and fuelled concern that partisan groups could exploit “rumblings” inside the ranks.
Armed Forces Chief of Staff General Romeo Brawner Jnr acknowledged unease among some personnel but told This Week in Asia that the military would remain out of politics.
“Political intervention is far from our mandate,” Brawner said

asia-pacific.title: Canada moves to ban under-16s from social media, regulate AI
asia-pacific.summary:
Canada’s culture minister on Wednesday introduced legislation that would ban children under 16 from having social media accounts and require AI chatbot services to limit production of harmful content.
The proposed Digital Safety Act makes Canada the latest in a wave of countries cracking down on social media platforms over concerns of harm to children.
“We have seen the very serious consequences that online harms can have … The safety of children cannot be an afterthought,” said Culture Minister
